source: NIST CFReDS Hacking Case (cfreds-archive.nist.gov/Hacking_Case.html). Community-validated answers from multiple independent forensic writeups. Enriched from VIGIA-REAL-001. Note: NIST does not publish an official answer key — answers cross-validated across independent sources.

attack_chains.name: System & User Identification
attack_chains.summary: Registry analysis identifies the suspect (Greg Schardt), computer name (N-1A9ODN6ZXK4LQ), user account (Mr. Evil), timezone, and system configuration.

attack_chains.name: Network Reconnaissance & War Driving
attack_chains.summary: Wireless network scanning tools (Network Stumbler) and network discovery tools (Look@LAN) used for active reconnaissance of WiFi networks. Packet captures of third-party traffic found.

attack_chains.name: Communication & Identity
attack_chains.summary: mIRC client configured with aliases (Mr, mrevilrulez), Yahoo Messenger account (mrevil2000), and email accounts (mrevilrulez@yahoo.com, whoknowsme@sbcglobal.net) establish the suspect's online identity and communication channels.

attack_chains.name: Credential Theft & Network Sniffing
attack_chains.summary: Ethereal/packet capture tools used to intercept network traffic and capture credentials from wireless networks.

findings.description: Computer name identified as N-1A9ODN6ZXK4LQ from registry SYSTEM\ControlSet001\Control\ComputerName
findings.notes: NIST Q2. Found in SYSTEM hive. Hostname N-1A9ODN6ZXK4LQ.

findings.description: Registered owner identified as Greg Schardt from SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\RegisteredOwner
findings.notes: NIST Q1. RegisteredOwner = Greg Schardt. Links physical identity to the device.

findings.description: User account Mr. Evil (SID S-1-5-21-2000478354-688789844-1708537768-1003) exists with last login evidence
findings.notes: NIST Q3/Q6/Q7. Mr. Evil account with known SID. Was last to log in. Has admin privileges (member of Administrators group).

findings.description: System timezone set to Central Standard Time (UTC-6)
findings.notes: NIST Q4. TimeZoneInformation in SYSTEM hive. Important for timestamp correlation.

findings.description: Network configuration shows IP 192.168.1.111 and MAC 00:10:a4:93:3e:09
findings.notes: NIST Q9/Q10. Found in SYSTEM\ControlSet001\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\Interfaces. MAC address 00:10:a4:93:3e:09 from network adapter config.

findings.description: Look@LAN configuration file C:\Program Files\Look@LAN\irunin.ini links Greg Schardt to alias Mr. Evil
findings.notes: NIST Q12. Critical finding: irunin.ini contains registration to Greg Schardt AND references Mr. Evil alias. This is the key link between real identity and hacker persona.

findings.description: mIRC configuration C:\Program Files\mIRC\mirc.ini shows user=Mini Me, nick=Mr, alternate nick=mrevilrulez
findings.notes: NIST Q13. mIRC installed and configured. Nick aliases connect to the Mr. Evil persona. Check mirc.ini [mirc] section for user, nick, anick fields.

findings.description: Network Stumbler (wireless network scanner/war driving tool) installed and used
findings.notes: NIST Q15. War driving tool for discovering wireless networks. Look in Program Files, prefetch, or registry Uninstall keys. May also check for .ns1 scan result files.

findings.description: Ethereal (packet capture/network sniffer) installed for intercepting network traffic
findings.notes: NIST Q14. Ethereal (predecessor to Wireshark) installed. Used for capturing packets on wireless networks. Check for .pcap/.cap files as evidence of actual use.

findings.description: Look@LAN network discovery tool installed for reconnaissance
findings.notes: NIST Q12. Network enumeration tool. The irunin.ini file within provides the identity link. Also check for scan results.

findings.description: Email account mrevilrulez@yahoo.com found in application configuration
findings.notes: VIGIA key_iocs. Found in email client or mIRC config. Connects the Mr. Evil alias to a communication channel. Also check Outlook Express .dbx files.

findings.description: Yahoo Messenger account mrevil2000 configured on the system
findings.notes: VIGIA key_iocs. Yahoo Messenger profile ID. Check registry SOFTWARE\Yahoo\Pager or Yahoo Messenger program data folder.

findings.description: Secondary email whoknowsme@sbcglobal.net found in email configuration
findings.notes: VIGIA key_iocs. Additional email account. Check Outlook Express identity folders and .dbx files.

findings.description: Last system shutdown time recorded in registry (Windows XP stores in SYSTEM\ControlSet001\Control\Windows\ShutdownTime)
findings.notes: NIST Q5. ShutdownTime value in Windows key. Binary value needs to be decoded as FILETIME. Establishes timeline boundary.

findings.description: Network packet capture files (.pcap or .cap) found containing intercepted third-party traffic
findings.notes: Evidence of actual network sniffing/interception activity. Look for .pcap, .cap, .eth files in user directories or Ethereal output folders. Proves tools were actually used, not just installed.

findings.description: File hash MD5 aee4fcd9301c03b3b054623ca261959a for key evidence file
findings.notes: VIGIA key_iocs. MD5 hash for verification/chain of custody. From ART-004 file metadata context.

findings.description: Wireless network scan results or war driving logs found (Network Stumbler output)
findings.notes: Network Stumbler saves .ns1 files with discovered SSIDs, BSSIDs, channels, signal strength. Proves active reconnaissance was conducted, not just that tools were installed.

findings.description: Windows SID S-1-5-21-2000478354-688789844-1708537768-1003 confirms Mr. Evil account identity
findings.notes: VIGIA key_iocs. The specific SID for Mr. Evil account. RID 1003 indicates third user account created. Correlates with SAM and ProfileList entries.
